proposals.title: Review and promote parsed runtime evidence into the source ledger.
proposals.rationale: The latest init, doctor, and eval runtime artifacts all parse through KRN contracts; a human should review before durable promotion.

proposals.title: Use selected memory IDs as review guidance before adding new product surfaces.
proposals.rationale: The MemoryStore boundary is the active final-product slice; selected memory must produce application guidance and feedback before it counts as product memory.

memory_selection.task_intent: Review current KRN runtime evidence against the active final-product goal.

selected.reason: Selected because the task targets repo-local memory-core and context-dump failure modes.
selected.expected_use: Reject repo-local memory-core claims and require selected memory IDs, rejected context, application guidance, and feedback.

rejected_context.reason: Expanded benchmark goals are lab/archive context and are rejected for default product review.
Full memory-bank scans are context dumps; this review may use only selected memory IDs.
.krn is runtime evidence/cache/ledger and must not be treated as authoritative memory.

retrieval_budget.selection_policy: active review records only; reject stale/lab/archive context and full memory-bank scans

memory_selection.overclaim_boundary: This selection proves bounded local-dev memory selection only.

blockers: Do not treat docs/memory/** or .krn/** as final memory core.
required_checks: Review output must cite selected memory IDs and rejected context.
review_questions: Did selected memory change the next review/action decision?
memory_application.next_action: Use this application guidance in krn review, then record pending feedback to the MemoryStore adapter.
memory_application.overclaim_boundary: This application proves review guidance wiring only.

memory_outcomes.reason: KRN applied this memory to review guidance; human/metric outcome is pending.

memory_feedback.notes: Feedback is usage evidence, not a productivity-lift claim.

interpretation_caveat: This report applies selected memory IDs to local review guidance only; it does not approve memory/source changes, store authoritative memory in the repo, prove productivity lift, or unblock destructive API/MCP/dashboard behavior by itself.
